args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

books_lit_file = var_call_SGaFmkXeZlw6zbMbhqynjxlX

with open(books_lit_file, 'r') as f:
    books_lit = json.load(f)

reviews = var_call_NNvqW7s9eEJfTBYcbAYeKcmt

books_df = pd.DataFrame(books_lit)
reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews)

# Keep only perfect-5.0 purchase_ids
perfect_ids = set(reviews_df['purchase_id'].unique())

# Fuzzy join assumption: purchase_id like 'purchaseid_101' <-> 'bookid_101'
# Map purchase_ids to corresponding book_ids
reviews_df['book_id'] = reviews_df['purchase_id'].str.replace('purchaseid_', 'bookid_', regex=False)

# Join with books in Literature & Fiction
merged = pd.merge(books_df, reviews_df[['book_id','avg_rating','num_reviews']].drop_duplicates(), on='book_id', how='inner')

# Filter English-language from details text
eng_mask = books_df['details'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False)
merged = merged[eng_mask.loc[merged.index].values]

# Select unique books
result_books = merged[['book_id','title','categories','details','avg_rating','num_reviews']].drop_duplicates().sort_values('title')

result = result_books.to_dict(orient='records')

out = json.dumps(result)
print("__RESULT__:")
print(out)

val_args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

books_lit_file = var_call_SGaFmkXeZlw6zbMbhqynjxlX

with open(books_lit_file, 'r') as f:
    books_lit = json.load(f)

reviews = var_call_NNvqW7s9eEJfTBYcbAYeKcmt

books_df = pd.DataFrame(books_lit)
reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews)

# Keep only perfect-5.0 purchase_ids
perfect_ids = set(reviews_df['purchase_id'].unique())

# Fuzzy join assumption: purchase_id like 'purchaseid_101' <-> 'bookid_101'
# Map purchase_ids to corresponding book_ids
reviews_df['book_id'] = reviews_df['purchase_id'].str.replace('purchaseid_', 'bookid_', regex=False)

# Join with books in Literature & Fiction
merged = pd.merge(books_df, reviews_df[['book_id','avg_rating','num_reviews']].drop_duplicates(), on='book_id', how='inner')

# Filter English-language from details text
eng_mask = books_df['details'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False)
merged = merged[eng_mask.loc[merged.index].values]

# Select unique books
result_books = merged[['book_id','title','categories','details','avg_rating','num_reviews']].drop_duplicates().sort_values('title')

result = result_books.to_dict(orient='records')

out = json.dumps(result)
print("__RESULT__:")
print(out)
